author: David Reed
summary: Phase H0 baseline crystallization. No behavioral change — this iteration establishes the instrumentation surface (OpenTelemetry spans on every agent call, structured trajectory logs, correlation-ID-linked HIPAA audit pairs) that subsequent iterations measure against. The pre-AHE codebase state is captured under tag pre-ahe-baseline; harness-iter-0 marks the first state where every behavioral path is observable end-to-end. Future iterations attribute their predicted-vs-observed deltas relative to this baseline.

changes.description: Establish baseline observability: OpenTelemetry tracer initialization, span emission on every agent execute() call, trajectory log writes alongside the HIPAA audit trail, and correlation-ID propagation through the orchestrator. No prompt, tool, memory, middleware, or eval-suite changes.
changes.failure_pattern: No failure pattern targeted. H0 is the precondition for all subsequent measurement; without it, iterations cannot produce falsifiable verdicts.
changes.root_cause: Pre-instrumentation, agent calls were observable only via the HIPAA audit trail, which captures decision-level events but not intra-agent steps. Phases H1-H5 require step-level visibility to attribute outcomes to specific harness-component changes.

evidence.note: Eleven tests verify span emission, retry-with-tracing semantics, and that trajectory logs contain one entry per agent call. All pass.
Documents the three observability pillars (component, experience, decision) the H0 instrumentation enables.

changes.why_this_component: Instrumentation is intentionally outside the seven AHE constraint levels because it must precede them. Adding tracing to a system whose components are already extracted creates retrofit problems and obscures attribution.
changes.rollback_plan: git revert <sha>. Reverting removes the tracer and trajectory-logger imports, leaving the HIPAA audit trail (which existed pre-H0) as the sole observability surface. No state migration required.
